I need the PWA team to implement cache-first with network validation fallback, add Cache-Control: no-cache for hashed assets, and validate cache freshness post-deploy.

I noticed our async form validators are causing race conditions and stale error states. When users type rapidly, the validation promise resolves out of order, overwriting the latest error with an older one. The console shows Warning: Can't perform a React state update on an unmounted component when navigating away during validation. I need the forms team to implement AbortController for pending validators, add request ID sequencing, and validate error state consistency under rapid input.

I was reviewing our Next.js middleware and found redirect loops on the auth route. The browser logs ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS because the middleware checks req.cookies.session but the cookie domain doesn't match .example.com. The middleware redirects to /login, which triggers itself again. I need the auth team to fix the cookie domain configuration, add recursive redirect guards, and validate session persistence across subdomains.

I noticed our CSS :has() selector is causing severe performance degradation on the data table. Chrome DevTools shows Layout > Recalculate Style: 142ms per row render because :has(.expanded) forces full DOM traversal on every mutation. This breaks smooth scrolling on large datasets. I need the UI team to replace :has() with JavaScript-driven class toggles, add will-change optimizations, and validate rendering performance with 10k+ rows.

I was debugging our Zustand persist middleware and found hydration mismatches with API data. The console logs Hydration mismatch detected: state.cart.items because the persisted local state loads synchronously while the API fetch resolves asynchronously, causing a flash of stale data. I need the state team to implement a hydration guard that waits for API sync before rehydration, add loading skeletons, and validate state merge order.

I noticed our Playwright route.abort() isn't triggering fallback UI correctly. The test throws Error: Route handler called abort() but request still pending when simulating network failures. Playwright's request interception doesn't propagate the abort event to the component's fetch error handler. I need the QA team to implement route.fulfill({ status: 500 }) instead of abort, add mock response factories, and validate error boundary rendering under network failure.

I was reviewing our Webpack optimization.minimize config and found it's breaking CSS custom properties. The production build outputs --var-primary: undefined because CSSNano incorrectly strips custom properties during minification. This breaks theme switching in production. I need the build team to disable discardUnused in CSSNano, add explicit postcss-custom-properties plugins, and validate theme variable retention in production builds.

I noticed our role='dialog' component is missing Escape key handling. Screen reader tests show focus remains trapped in the modal with no keyboard exit, violating WCAG 2.1 2.1.2. The event listener isn't attached to the document root. I need the a11y team to add global keydown listener for Escape, return focus to trigger element on close, and validate keyboard navigation with NVDA/JAWS.

I was debugging our fetch implementation and found keepalive: true dropping request headers on mobile Safari. The network inspector shows Origin: null and missing Authorization headers when sending POST with keepalive: true. Safari strips headers for keepalive requests to prevent CORS bypass. I need the networking team to implement fallback to standard fetch on iOS Safari, add header validation, and validate cross-browser request integrity.

I noticed our Next.js Image component placeholders aren't aligning with final loaded images. The layout shift score jumps to 0.28 because placeholder='blur' doesn't match the object-fit scaling of the final image. This causes visual jank on the hero section. I need the UI team to implement aspect-ratio containers with background-size: cover, add smooth crossfade transitions, and validate layout stability with Lighthouse CI.

I was reviewing our CSS container queries and found container-type: inline-size causing overflow issues on mobile. The container clips content unexpectedly when width: 100% exceeds the parent's padding box. Chrome DevTools shows overflow: hidden applied by the container query context. I need the styling team to add explicit box-sizing: border-box to containers, remove conflicting overflow rules, and validate mobile layout containment.

I noticed our React useId hook is generating mismatched IDs between server and client when using dynamic routing. The console logs Warning: useId() returned "r12" on server but "r13" on client because the component tree order changes based on route params. This breaks form label associations. I need the frontend team to implement deterministic ID generators based on route keys, add stable component ordering guards, and validate ID consistency across server/client rendering.

I was debugging our Playwright screenshot tests and found flaky expect(locator).toHaveScreenshot() failures. The diff shows expected: button-blue, actual: button-purple because the theme toggle persists between test runs via localStorage. The test environment isn't resetting storage state. I need the QA team to implement localStorage.clear() in beforeEach, add theme state isolation, and validate visual regression stability across test suites.

I noticed our Webpack externals configuration isn't excluding Node builtins correctly. The browser console throws Uncaught ReferenceError: require is not defined when importing a shared utility that uses fs and path. Webpack's externals regex isn't matching the CommonJS require calls. I need the build team to implement node: false in webpack config, add explicit browser field overrides in package.json, and validate browser compatibility for shared libs.

I was debugging our focus management and found it breaking on dynamic content loads. When the product grid loads more items via infinite scroll, focus jumps to the first item in the new batch instead of staying on the 'Load More' button. This violates WCAG 2.1 3.2.3. I need the a11y team to implement focus preservation hooks, add aria-live announcements for dynamic loads, and validate focus flow under infinite scroll.

I noticed our IndexedDB quota is being exceeded on media-heavy pages. The browser throws QuotaExceededError: Failed to execute 'put' on 'IDBObjectStore': Storage quota exceeded after caching ~500MB of images. The cache eviction policy isn't removing expired entries. I need the storage team to implement LRU cache eviction, add quota monitoring alerts, and validate storage cleanup under heavy media usage.

I was reviewing our CORS configuration and found Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * blocking credential requests. The browser console logs CORS policy: The value of the 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header in the response must not be the wildcard '*' when the request's credentials mode is 'include'. Our API is rejecting auth requests from staging. I need the backend team to replace wildcard with explicit origin reflection, add Vary: Origin headers, and validate CORS compliance with credentials enabled.

I noticed our React.lazy fallback component isn't unmounting when the chunk loads. React DevTools shows the fallback component lingering in the tree, causing memory leaks and unnecessary re-renders. The Suspense boundary isn't properly cleaning up the fallback state. I need the frontend team to verify Suspense implementation, add explicit unmount guards, and validate component lifecycle under chunk loading.

I was debugging our CSS-in-JS global style injection and found duplicate <style> tags being appended on every route change. The DOM shows 40+ duplicate <style> elements with identical content, increasing memory usage and causing style precedence conflicts. The createGlobalStyle component isn't being memoized. I need the styling team to implement useMemo for global style components, add injection deduplication logic, and validate DOM cleanliness during navigation.

I noticed our Jest fakeTimers aren't advancing correctly in async tests. The test runner throws Error: expect(received).toBe(expected) // Expected: 2 // Received: 0 because jest.advanceTimersByTime(1000) doesn't trigger setTimeout callbacks inside async/await blocks. I need the testing team to use jest.runAllTimersAsync(), verify timer advancement in async contexts, and validate timer-based logic under async execution.

I was debugging our virtual scroll container and found height miscalculations after window resize. The container height doesn't recalculate when window.innerWidth changes, causing items to overlap and scroll offsets to misalign. The ResizeObserver callback isn't updating the scroll container's computed dimensions. I need the UI team to implement proper resize debouncing, recalculate item heights on viewport change, and validate scroll alignment across responsive breakpoints.

I noticed our Service Worker fetch event is incorrectly intercepting WebSocket connections. The browser logs DOMException: Failed to execute 'send' on 'WebSocket': The connection is not in the OPEN state because the SW respondWith handler wraps WebSocket upgrade requests in a Response object. I need the PWA team to add WebSocket detection in the fetch handler, bypass SW interception for ws:// and wss:// protocols, and validate WebSocket connectivity with SW enabled.

I was debugging our async form validation and found stale error states persisting after field correction. When users fix an invalid email, the old error message remains visible until another field loses focus. The validation state isn't resetting on value change. I need the forms team to implement real-time validation clearing on onChange, add error state reset logic, and validate UX consistency during form correction.

I noticed our Next.js getServerSideProps is caching responses incorrectly on the edge network. The CDN returns X-Cache: HIT for dynamic user-specific data, causing users to see other users' profiles. The cache headers aren't setting Cache-Control: private, no-store. I need the backend team to configure edge cache bypass for authenticated routes, add explicit no-cache headers, and validate data isolation across CDN nodes.

I was reviewing our CSS subgrid implementation and found it breaking on Firefox 120. The layout collapses because Firefox doesn't support grid-template-rows: subgrid inside flex containers. The fallback isn't activating due to incorrect @supports syntax. I need the styling team to implement grid-template-rows: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)) fallback, fix @supports condition syntax, and validate cross-browser grid compatibility.

I noticed our Zustand persist hydration is racing with API data fetches. The console logs Warning: An update to App inside a test was not wrapped in act(...) because local storage rehydration overwrites API-fetched state before it's ready. This causes flickering and data inconsistency on load. I need the state team to implement a hydration lock that waits for API readiness, add state merge precedence rules, and validate load sequence under network latency.

I was debugging our Playwright route mocking and found route.abort() not triggering component fallbacks correctly. The test fails with TimeoutError: locator.waitFor because the component's error handler isn't catching the abort event properly. Playwright's network interception doesn't simulate real network errors accurately. I need the QA team to implement route.fulfill({ status: 404, body: '{}' }) for error simulation, add error boundary testing, and validate fallback rendering under mocked failures.

I noticed our Webpack optimization is minifying CSS variables incorrectly. The production build outputs --primary: undefined because PostCSS incorrectly evaluates CSS custom properties during minification. This breaks theme switching in production. I need the build team to disable cssnano's discardUnused and reduceIdents, add explicit custom property preservation config, and validate theme variable retention post-build.

I was debugging our modal accessibility and found missing Escape key handling. Keyboard users can't close modals without using a mouse, violating WCAG 2.1 2.1.1. The global keydown listener isn't attached to the document root. I need the a11y team to add document-level keydown listener for Escape, implement focus return to trigger element, and validate keyboard navigation with screen readers.

I noticed our fetch implementation with keepalive: true is dropping custom headers on iOS Safari. The network inspector shows Authorization header missing when sending POST requests. Safari strips headers for keepalive requests to prevent CORS bypass. I need the networking team to implement platform-specific fetch fallbacks, add header validation before sending, and validate cross-browser request integrity.

I was profiling our Next.js image placeholders and found layout shifts on hero sections. Lighthouse shows CLS: 0.31 because placeholder aspect ratio doesn't match final image scaling. This causes visual jank during page load. I need the UI team to implement precise aspect-ratio containers, add smooth crossfade transitions, and validate layout stability under image loading conditions.

I noticed our CSS container queries are causing overflow clipping on mobile. The container-type: inline-size applies overflow: hidden by default, clipping dropdowns and tooltips. Chrome DevTools shows overflow: hidden applied to container parent. I need the styling team to add explicit overflow: visible to containers, remove conflicting overflow rules, and validate mobile layout containment with interactive elements.

I was debugging our React useId mismatches and found inconsistent ID generation across server/client renders. The console logs Hydration mismatch: useId() returned different IDs because dynamic route params change component tree order. This breaks form accessibility. I need the frontend team to implement deterministic ID generators based on stable route keys, add component ordering validation, and validate ID consistency across hydration.

I noticed our Playwright screenshot tests are flaky due to theme persistence between runs. The visual diff shows color mismatches because localStorage theme state isn't cleared. I need the QA team to implement beforeEach storage clearing, add theme state isolation, and validate visual regression stability across test suites.

I was reviewing our Webpack externals config and found Node builtins leaking into browser bundles. The console throws require is not defined when importing shared utilities with fs references. Webpack's externals regex isn't matching CommonJS requires. I need the build team to configure node: false, add browser field overrides, and validate browser compatibility for shared libraries.

I noticed our focus management breaks during infinite scroll. Focus jumps to newly loaded items instead of staying on the 'Load More' button. This violates WCAG 3.2.3. I need the a11y team to implement focus preservation hooks, add aria-live announcements for dynamic loads, and validate focus flow under infinite scroll.

I was debugging our IndexedDB quota and found storage limits being exceeded on media-heavy pages. The browser throws QuotaExceededError after caching ~500MB of images. The eviction policy isn't removing expired entries. I need the storage team to implement LRU cache eviction, add quota monitoring, and validate storage cleanup under heavy usage.

I noticed our CORS configuration blocks credential requests due to wildcard origin. The browser logs Access-Control-Allow-Origin must not be '*' when credentials mode is 'include'. Our API rejects auth requests from staging. I need the backend team to replace wildcard with explicit origin reflection, add Vary: Origin headers, and validate CORS compliance.

I was debugging our React.lazy fallback components and found memory leaks. React DevTools shows fallbacks lingering in the tree after chunks load. The Suspense boundary isn't cleaning up fallback state. I need the frontend team to verify Suspense implementation, add unmount guards, and validate component lifecycle under chunk loading.

I noticed our CSS-in-JS global style injection creates duplicate <style> tags on route changes. The DOM shows 40+ duplicate elements, increasing memory usage and causing precedence conflicts. The global style component isn't memoized. I need the styling team to implement useMemo, add injection deduplication, and validate DOM cleanliness during navigation.

I was debugging our Jest fake timers and found async test failures. The test runner throws expected 2, received 0 because advanceTimersByTime doesn't trigger async setTimeout callbacks. I need the testing team to use runAllTimersAsync(), verify timer advancement in async contexts, and validate timer logic under async execution.

I noticed our virtual scroll container height miscalculates after window resize. Items overlap and scroll offsets misalign because ResizeObserver isn't updating computed dimensions. I need the UI team to implement resize debouncing, recalculate item heights on viewport change, and validate scroll alignment across breakpoints.

I was debugging our Service Worker and found it intercepting WebSocket connections incorrectly. The browser logs WebSocket connection errors because SW respondWith wraps upgrade requests. I need the PWA team to add WebSocket detection, bypass SW interception for ws:// protocols, and validate WebSocket connectivity with SW enabled.

I noticed our async form validation leaves stale errors visible after field correction. Old errors persist until another field loses focus. I need the forms team to implement real-time validation clearing on onChange, add error state reset logic, and validate UX consistency during correction.

I was reviewing our Next.js edge caching and found cached responses serving user-specific data incorrectly. CDN returns HIT for dynamic profiles. I need the backend team to configure edge cache bypass for authenticated routes, add no-cache headers, and validate data isolation across CDN nodes.

I noticed our CSS subgrid breaks on Firefox 120 due to flex container incompatibility. Fallback isn't activating due to incorrect @supports syntax. I need the styling team to implement fallback grid rules, fix @supports condition syntax, and validate cross-browser compatibility.

I was debugging our Zustand hydration race with API fetches. Local storage rehydration overwrites API data before it's ready, causing flickering. I need the state team to implement hydration lock, add state merge precedence, and validate load sequence under network latency.

I noticed our Playwright route mocking doesn't trigger component fallbacks correctly with route.abort(). Test fails with timeout because error handler isn't catching abort. I need the QA team to implement route.fulfill for error simulation, add error boundary testing, and validate fallback rendering.

I was reviewing our Webpack CSS minification and found variables being minified to undefined. Production build breaks theme switching. I need the build team to disable cssnano discards, add custom property preservation, and validate theme retention post-build.

I noticed our modal accessibility misses Escape key handling. Keyboard users can't close modals, violating WCAG. I need the a11y team to add document-level keydown listener, implement focus return, and validate keyboard navigation.

I was debugging our fetch keepalive implementation and found headers dropping on iOS Safari. Network inspector shows Authorization header missing. I need the networking team to implement platform-specific fetch fallbacks, add header validation, and validate cross-browser integrity.

I noticed our Next.js image placeholders cause layout shifts on hero sections. Lighthouse shows CLS 0.31. I need the UI team to implement precise aspect-ratio containers, add smooth transitions, and validate layout stability.

I was reviewing our CSS container queries and found overflow clipping on mobile. Container applies overflow hidden by default, clipping dropdowns. I need the styling team to add explicit overflow visible, remove conflicting rules, and validate mobile layout.

I noticed our React useId mismatches across hydration due to dynamic route params changing component tree order. I need the frontend team to implement deterministic ID generators, add ordering validation, and validate ID consistency.

I was troubleshooting a 502 Bad Gateway spike on our Nginx reverse proxy after the latest TLS certificate rotation. The error logs show SSL_do_handshake() failed (SSL: error:0A000086:SSL routines::certificate verify failed). OCSP stapling is failing because the ssl_trusted_certificate directive points to an expired intermediate chain. This is breaking HTTPS for ~35% of inbound traffic. I need the infrastructure team to update the intermediate CA bundle, verify ssl_stapling on, and run nginx -t before reloading.

I noticed our custom app-worker.service stuck in activating (auto-restart) loop after the systemd upgrade. journalctl -u app-worker shows ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/worker exited with code 127. The symlink was wiped during /usr/local cleanup. Background job processing is completely halted. I need the platform team to restore the binary path or use absolute paths in the unit file, run systemctl daemon-reload, and verify service startup.

I discovered /var partition is at 100% inode usage despite having 40% disk space free. df -i shows millions of 0-byte temp files created by a runaway log rotation script that lacks maxage configuration. Services are crashing with No space left on device when trying to create PID or lock files. I need the sysadmin team to run find /var/tmp -type f -mtime +2 -delete, fix the logrotate config, and set inode monitoring alerts.

I noticed PostgreSQL hitting the max_connections=500 limit during peak hours. pg_stat_activity reveals 420 connections stuck in idle state from an unconfigured connection pooler. New application requests are rejected with FATAL: too many connections for role "app_user". I need the DBA team to tune PgBouncer max_client_conn, implement client_idle_timeout, and verify connection recycling under load.

I was debugging internal DNS resolution and found our staging subdomains returning NXDOMAIN after the BIND migration. named-checkzone shows missing zone delegation for staging.internal.corp. CI/CD pipelines are failing due to unreachable registry endpoints. I need the network team to restore the zone file, run rndc reload, and verify propagation across resolver caches.

I noticed the Java application server being OOM-killed every 4 hours. dmesg shows Out of memory: Killed process 8421 (java) total-vm:16GB, anon-rss:14GB. Thread dump analysis reveals an unbounded HTTP/2 stream cache leaking memory. API response times degrade severely before each restart. I need the backend team to patch the stream multiplexer config, enable G1GC with MaxGCPauseMillis, and add JVM heap dump auto-rotation.

I discovered iptables rules dropping established connections after a firewall policy reload. conntrack -L shows state mismatches because iptables-restore flushed the connection tracking table. Active SSH sessions and long-polling API requests are severed. I need the network team to switch to nftables for stateful rule application or implement conntrack preservation during reloads.

I noticed our software RAID 1 array is degraded. cat /proc/mdstat shows md2 : active raid1 sda2[0] sdb2[_] with one disk marked removed. smartctl reports Reallocated_Sector_Ct warnings on /dev/sdb. We've lost disk redundancy and risk complete storage failure. I need the storage team to hot-swap the failing drive, trigger mdadm --add, and monitor rebuild progress.

I was investigating JWT authentication failures and found Chrony drifting by -6.2 seconds across the cluster. chronyc tracking shows System time: -6.234s offset. Token validation fails across microservices due to time mismatch, and TLS handshakes reject connections. I need the infra team to sync to pool.ntp.org, adjust maxpoll 10, restart chronyd, and verify NTP stratum alignment.

I noticed HAProxy health checks failing due to TCP FIN_WAIT_2 accumulation. netstat -an | grep FIN shows 12k+ connections stuck in half-closed state. Traffic is being routed to backends that appear healthy but are actually unresponsive, resulting in 503 errors. I need the platform team to tune timeout close, enable option httpclose, and adjust net.ipv4.tcp_fin_timeout via sysctl.

I discovered app-database-migrate.service failing to start on boot with dependency failed for multi-user.target. The systemd unit has conflicting After= and Requires= directives for network-online.target. DB migrations don't run, and the app starts against an outdated schema. I need the DevOps team to fix the unit file ordering, run systemctl daemon-reload, and verify migration execution on startup.

I noticed a custom Netfilter kernel module failing to load after the latest kernel update. insmod netfilter_custom.ko returns Unknown symbol in module. Custom firewall rules aren't applied, leaving internal APIs exposed to unfiltered traffic. I need the kernel team to recompile the module against linux-headers-$(uname -r), install via DKMS, and verify module signing.

I noticed VM CPU throttling at 25% due to hypervisor cgroup limits. cat /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.max shows quota 25000 period 100000. API response times spike during peak load, and background workers time out. I need the cloud team to adjust CPU quota/period ratios, evaluate dedicated host migration, and monitor cpu.stat throttling metrics.

I discovered SELinux denying httpd access to our custom upload directory. audit.log shows AVC denied { read write } for comm="httpd" path="/data/uploads". File uploads return 403 Forbidden despite correct POSIX permissions. I need the security team to generate a custom policy module with audit2allow, apply it, and verify semanage fcontext persistence.

I noticed cron jobs running 2 hours late after the DST transition. The server timezone is UTC but the cron daemon inherited Europe/Berlin from legacy environment files. Backup windows and report generation are misaligned with SLA windows. I need the sysadmin team to export TZ=UTC in /etc/cron.d, restart crond, and validate execution times.

I noticed a long-running process hitting Too many open files (EMFILE) limits. ulimit -n shows 1024, but the application requires 65k for high-concurrency WebSocket handling. Socket connections fail, causing API degradation. I need the platform team to set LimitNOFILE=65536 in the systemd service override, reload the daemon, and verify lsof counts.

I discovered our local DNS resolver stuck in a recursive loop. named-checkconf shows misconfigured forwarders pointing back to the local resolver. All internal and external DNS resolution fails, breaking service discovery and external API calls. I need the network team to correct forwarder IPs, disable local recursion if unnecessary, and restart BIND.

I noticed high iowait on /dev/sda. iostat -x 1 shows 98% utilization with await at 120ms. A stale LVM snapshot is causing severe write amplification on the database volume. Database queries timeout, and replication lag increases. I need the storage team to merge or remove the snapshot, tune I/O scheduler to mq-deadline, and monitor disk latency.

I discovered SSH logins blocked by pam_nologin.so after a maintenance window. /etc/nologin was left behind by a deployment script. Engineering and SRE teams are locked out of production servers. I need the sysadmin team to remove /etc/nologin, verify PAM configuration, and implement automated cleanup post-maintenance.

I noticed packet loss on cross-AZ traffic due to MTU mismatch. ping -M do -s 1472 fails while 1400 succeeds. Jumbo frames enabled on host NIC but disabled on upstream switch. Large API payloads fragment and drop, breaking file sync services. I need the network team to align MTU on switch ports, adjust host MTU, and validate path MTU discovery.

I noticed systemd socket activation failing to trigger the app service. ss -ltnp shows the socket bound on port 8080, but systemctl status app.service shows inactive. Lazy-loaded microservice doesn't start on demand, causing cold-start timeouts. I need the platform team to fix Accept=yes in the socket unit, verify Type=notify in service, and test socket activation flow.

I noticed Redis using 96% of allocated memory and triggering maxmemory-policy allkeys-random eviction. INFO memory shows active eviction. User session keys are being dropped randomly, causing unexpected logouts and cart abandonment. I need the cache team to increase maxmemory, switch to allkeys-lru, and audit key TTL distribution.

I discovered a kernel panic triggered by a buggy e1000e NIC driver. kdump shows kernel BUG at drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/netdev.c. Server reboots unexpectedly under high network load, causing service interruptions. I need the kernel team to apply upstream driver patch, disable TSO offload as workaround, and schedule kernel update.

I noticed nf_conntrack table full. dmesg shows nf_conntrack: table full, dropping packet. New TCP connections are silently dropped, making the load balancer appear down to clients. I need the network team to increase nf_conntrack_max to 524288, tune connection timeouts, and investigate connection leak sources.

I discovered DNSSEC validation failing due to expired root trust anchor. named logs DNSSEC validation failed: bad cache hit (DNSKEY). Secure domains like .gov and .bank unresolvable, breaking compliance API integrations. I need the DNS team to update managed-keys with latest root KSK, restart BIND, and verify dig +dnssec validation.

I noticed LVM logical volume extended but filesystem not resized. lvextend completed but resize2fs was skipped in the automation script. Applications report disk full and fail to write logs or temp files. I need the storage team to run resize2fs /dev/mapper/vg-lv (or xfs_growfs), verify df -h, and add filesystem resize step to IaC.

I noticed Nginx proxy buffering causing 504 Gateway Timeout for long-polling endpoints. proxy_read_timeout set to 30s, but upstream maintains connections for 5 minutes. Buffer fills up, connection drops, real-time features break. I need the platform team to disable buffering for /api/stream, increase proxy_read_timeout, and validate SSE/WebSocket stability.

I discovered journald logs consuming 78% of /var/log/journal. journalctl --disk-usage shows 14GB unrotated logs. Log rotation daemon failing due to incorrect SystemMaxUse config. I need the sysadmin team to set SystemMaxUse=5G in journald.conf, run journalctl --vacuum-size=2G, and restart journald.

I noticed SSH key authentication failing after openssh-server upgrade. sshd -T shows missing PubkeyAcceptedAlgorithms. RSA-SHA1 keys rejected by default, breaking legacy automation scripts and developer access. I need the security team to add PubkeyAcceptedAlgorithms +ssh-rsa (or migrate to ED25519), test key auth, and reload sshd.

I discovered ARP cache poisoning on the core switch. arp -a shows duplicate MAC addresses for the default gateway IP. Intermittent connectivity loss, packet drops, and security risk of MITM traffic redirection. I need the network team to enable Dynamic ARP Inspection (DAI) on access ports, isolate compromised endpoint, and verify ARP tables.
